title: The lifecycle of card disputes
summary:
Learn about the lifecycle of a card disputes so you can know what to expect and how to respond effectively. This video walks through what happens when a dispute is filed, your options for handling disputes, and possible disputes outcomes. Get started with Stripe today and access tools to help you prevent and manage disputes.

00:00 Introducing the lifecycle of a card disputes
00:09 The beginning of a dispute
00:43 Options for responding to a dispute
01:17 After evidence submission
02:11 Stripe solutions for disputes

Resources: https://docs.stripe.com/disputes/how-disputes-work

Get started with Stripe: https://stripe.com/register
transcript_html:
YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FQp9_xPlTmU

Introducing the lifecycle of a card disputes

In this video, we'll go over the life cycle and key events of card disputes so that you know what to expect. A dispute begins when a card holder

The beginning of a dispute

questions a charge with their issuing bank, which they can typically do up to 120 days after the purchase, though this varies based on the issuer and purchase type. When the dispute is filed, the card network debits Stripe for the disputed payment amount and related dispute fees, and your dispute rate with the card network increases. Stripe then debits your Stripe balance for the disputed amount plus a fee for receiving the dispute. Stripe will also notify you that you have received a dispute. You then have the option to accept this dispute or try to counter

Options for responding to a dispute

the dispute by submitting evidence, [music] which includes a fee for manually countering the dispute. This fee is returned to you if you win the dispute. [music] In the Stripe dashboard, you'll see the deadline, usually 7 to 21 days. This depends on the issuer's timeline, so be sure to check how much time you have before the deadline. You'll also see the status of the dispute, starting with needs response. Submit evidence before the deadline and the status will change to under review. Once submitted, the issuer reviews the evidence. Decisions

After evidence submission

typically take 60 to 75 days depending on the card network. A dispute can be won or lost. If the dispute is won, funds and dispute counter fees are returned. If you [music] lose, the card holder keeps the disputed amount. No matter if the dispute is won or lost, the dispute counts toward your dispute rate with the card issuer and the dispute received fee is not returned. Card holders can choose to withdraw a dispute, even if the dispute is lost. [music] If a customer withdraws a

dispute, we recommend you provide a letter of withdrawal to the issuer. You can still lose the dispute even if it's withdrawn, and it'll still count towards your dispute rate with the card issuer. Timely, organized responses and comprehensive evidence improve your chances of winning disputes. Stripe also offers solutions to help you manage

Stripe solutions for disputes

disputes. Dispute prevention powered by network solutions verify and etha helps you proactively resolve disputes so they don't count towards your dispute rate. And smart disputes uses AI to counter disputes on your behalf. For more details, check out the Stripe docs.
